summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of an electrifying Maine show, highlighted by a tear‑jerking Sugaree and a soaring Fire on the Mountain.
review: The recording sounds as if you’re standing right in front of the board – crisp, balanced and full of the summer‑day energy that made this concert one of the band’s finest. Reviewers rave about the emotional power of Sugaree and the fire‑breathing Fire on the Mountain, while noting an unusually forward Phil Lesh bass that adds a unique flavor. Overall both performance and tape quality are praised at the highest level.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews give 5‑star ratings, consistently praising both the musical performance and the soundboard recording; no significant criticisms were raised.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the audio as crystal‑clear, front‑of‑the‑board with crisp detail; a few note an unusually loud bass on Fire on the Mountain but overall mix is praised.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High energy throughout, with an especially emotional second‑half; the band’s interplay feels tight and the crowd’s enthusiasm is palpable.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Implied praise – “the boys were all over it” suggests Jerry’s lead guitar drove the excitement.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass was notably loud on Fire on the Mountain, which some reviewers found odd but added a distinctive edge.
